title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a behavior-and-type regression suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Behavior Type Tests · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

Which action demonstrates Behavior Type Tests without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.

content.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Behavior Type Tests result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A type test proves the emitted JavaScript behaves correctly.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Behavior Type Tests evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Behavior Type Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Type Complexity Restraint: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"6" that must not enter the trusted model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.
